hyperedges.label: JWT authentication flow (security + dependencies + config)
Reputation/trust recalculation pipeline driven by Review records
Core relational domain models built on SQLAlchemy Base
Per-router duplicated game-slug-to-404 + dynamic validation pattern
Review eligibility validation chain (search completed, double confirmation, time window, no duplicate)
Search join/accept/reject/full lifecycle pattern
Search participation/management UI flow
Authentication flow: token storage, context, and protected routing
Custom data-fetching hook pattern shared across lib hooks
GameProfile CRUD flow across pages
Post-game review pipeline: confirm played -> pending reviews -> write review
Search lifecycle state machine (open -> full -> in_progress -> completed/cancelled)
Anti-troll review pipeline (double confirmation, time window, weighting, outlier detection)
Search-to-review end-to-end flow across phases
Render deploy pipeline (build, migrate, seed, start)

nodes.label: JSON columns for game roles/servers/modes flexibility
nodes.norm_label: json columns for game roles/servers/modes flexibility

nodes.label: Search server must match creator's GameProfile server
nodes.norm_label: search server must match creator's gameprofile server

nodes.label: URL query params as filter state pattern
nodes.norm_label: url query params as filter state pattern

nodes.label: graphify knowledge graph workflow
nodes.rationale: Provides scoped subgraph queries (graphify query/path/explain) that are usually much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output, making codebase navigation faster.

nodes.label: Fase 2: Autenticación
nodes.rationale: Adds JWT-based stateless auth (register/login), bcrypt password hashing pinned to 4.0.1 to avoid a passlib 1.7.4 compatibility bug, generic login error messages to prevent user enumeration, and public/private user schema separation (UserOut hides email) so only the profile owner sees private data.

nodes.label: bcrypt==4.0.1 pin
nodes.rationale: bcrypt 4.1+ has a compatibility bug with passlib==1.7.4; pinning the version is the standard workaround until passlib releases a fix.

nodes.label: OAuth2PasswordBearer login flow
nodes.rationale: /auth/login uses form-data instead of JSON because it is the OAuth2 standard and makes the Swagger 'Authorize' button work without extra config.

nodes.label: Fase 3: Perfiles de juego por usuario
nodes.rationale: Introduces GameProfile as an intermediate table with its own attributes (roles, rank, server) rather than a plain join table, enabling dynamic role/server validation read from DB instead of hardcoded regex, and cross-field validation (main_role must be in roles) via Pydantic @model_validator.
nodes.norm_label: fase 3: perfiles de juego por usuario

nodes.label: Dynamic vs static validation
nodes.rationale: Validation rules depend on DB seed data, not hardcoded regex, so adding a new role to a game in the seed automatically adjusts validation without code changes.

nodes.label: Decision to defer Alembic to a later phase
nodes.rationale: No real data exists yet (all test data), so dropping and regenerating the DB is faster and more didactic than configuring migrations prematurely.
nodes.norm_label: decision to defer alembic to a later phase

nodes.label: Fase 4.5: Alembic migrations infrastructure
nodes.rationale: Replaces ad-hoc create_all/drop schema resets with versioned, reversible Alembic migrations so dev/staging/prod run identical migration history without losing data; introduces 'stamping' to adopt an existing DB without recreating tables.

nodes.label: Alembic stamping technique
nodes.rationale: Marks an existing DB (created via create_all in earlier phases) as already at the head migration, avoiding Alembic trying to recreate tables that already exist.

nodes.label: Fase 4: Sistema de búsquedas (LFG)
nodes.rationale: Implements a Search state machine (open -> full -> in_progress -> completed), cross-resource validation against the user's GameProfile, creator-only permission checks (403 vs 401), soft-delete via cancelled status, join_mode-driven auto-acceptance, and automatic creator-as-accepted-participant snapshot at creation time.

nodes.label: Search state machine
nodes.rationale: Explicit transitions (open -> full -> in_progress -> completed) prevent skipping states, e.g. cannot complete without first being in_progress.

nodes.label: Fase 5: Confirmación de partidas y reviews anti-troll
nodes.rationale: Closes the MVP: double-confirmation prevents unilateral phantom reviews, weight is calculated at review creation time (snapshot semantics) so later trust changes don't retroactively alter past reviews, 7-day time window prevents late revenge reviews, weighted average (not simple average) reflects reviewer trust, and outlier detection needs a baseline of prior reviews before flagging.
nodes.norm_label: fase 5: confirmacion de partidas y reviews anti-troll

nodes.label: Double confirmation anti-troll barrier
nodes.rationale: No unilateral system prevents phantom reviews; requiring both parties to confirm is the minimum safeguard.

nodes.label: Snapshot semantics for review weight
nodes.rationale: Weight is computed at review creation; later trust changes for the author don't retroactively alter the weight of already-written reviews, preserving history.

nodes.label: Outlier detection for reviews
nodes.rationale: Requires a baseline minimum number of prior reviews before a new review can be flagged as a statistical outlier; cannot flag from the very first review.

nodes.label: Primer arranque del proyecto (getting started guide)
nodes.norm_label: primer arranque del proyecto (getting started guide)

nodes.label: Anti-troll system (5 barriers)
nodes.rationale: Combines double confirmation, a 7-day review time window, mandatory 30+ character comments, trust-score-weighted reviews, and outlier detection (with 5+ prior reviews) to deter toxic/fraudulent reviews.

nodes.label: GameProfile separate-per-user-per-game design
nodes.rationale: A player can be Mid in LAS for League of Legends and AWPer in SA for Counter Strike; modeling GameProfile as an intermediate table between User and Game lets each profile have independent roles/server/rank without duplication, preserving referential integrity.

nodes.label: SQLite-first then PostgreSQL decision
nodes.rationale: SQLite is a zero-config file, perfect for development; in production only DATABASE_URL changes and SQLAlchemy/Alembic handle the rest without touching application code.

nodes.label: URL-based search filters decision
nodes.rationale: Filters like /searches?game_slug=...&server=... are shareable and reload-safe; keeping filters only in React state would break both reload persistence and link sharing.

nodes.label: Render + Vercel split infrastructure decision
nodes.rationale: Render hosts the Python backend plus managed PostgreSQL; Vercel hosts the static frontend with global CDN — standard separation where each platform is optimized for its role, both with generous free tiers and auto-deploy on push.
